name: Wang et al. High-Stakes Synthetic Off-Topic Detection Extract
population_or_context: Large-scale high-stakes English speaking assessment for non-native speakers; operational spoken responses elicited by 283 independent questions.
sample_size: 183,111 operational spoken responses split into 120,115 training and 62,996 test responses; synthetic off-topic examples were sampled to create 1:1 on-topic/off-topic partitions.
methods: Operational spoken responses were transcribed and compared with prompt texts using similarity grids and Inception networks. Synthetic off-topic cases were created by treating responses to other prompts as off-topic for a given prompt.
education_context: High-stakes spoken English proficiency assessment with spontaneous spoken responses.
response_origin: Authentic operational spoken responses plus synthetic off-topic examples created by mismatching real responses from other prompts.
rubric_label_space: Binary on-topic versus off-topic detection label for downstream scoring-pipeline protection.
label_source: Operational responses were originally scored by expert human raters; synthetic off-topic labels are induced by prompt-response mismatch rather than independent human scoring of authentic off-topic cases.
generation_model: Not applicable; synthetic cases are generated by mismatch sampling from real operational responses.
generation_method: For each target question, randomly select responses elicited by other questions and treat them as off-topic responses for the target question.
evaluation_method: Train and test off-topic detection models against synthetic off-topic and operational on-topic partitions using F1 for the off-topic class.
scorer_use: Synthetic off-topic responses support detection model development to inform downstream automated scoring; they do not validate the scoring model itself.
real_response_comparator: The on-topic corpus consists of operational real responses; authentic off-topic examples are described as rare, so the off-topic corpus is synthetic/mismatched.
reported_metrics: Inception-ResNet-v2 with idf channels reached F1 = 92.8% on the off-topic class, compared with a baseline F1 = 85.5%.
limitations: Synthetic off-topic examples are mismatched real responses, not LLM-generated student responses.
The workflow is detection and validity protection, not automated scoring validation.
Mismatched responses may not cover all authentic gaming, memorization, or off-topic response behaviors in live administrations.
